title: Key concepts: Human Migration
description: Test your knowledge of what contributes to human migrations!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the best definition of human migration?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: People who migrate from one place to another have a goal in mind.

choices.content: Movement to a new area with the intent to settle

assessment_items.question_content:
**What are factors that contribute to people’s decisions to _leave_ a particular area called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Pull factors are relevant in the end location of a migration.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What are factors that contribute to people’s decisions to _move to_ a particular area called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Push factors are relevant at the starting location of a migration.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following factors most strongly influenced Bantu migration patterns?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Bantu-speakers brought high-level ironworking technology with them as they moved.

choices.content: Whether the climate was suited to their agricultural practices
Whether there was available transportation technology
Whether they could avoid conflict with people possessing better technology

assessment_items.question_content:
**What made migration in the Pacific possible?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To travel in the Pacific required crossing long-distances over the ocean.

choices.content: An environment that allowed for large-scale agriculture
Transportation technology that allowed for long-distance ocean travel
The short distances between islands suitable for settlement
